scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[1] Start the result from the second row
scenarios.description: [1] Start the result from the second row
scenarios.query:
MATCH (n)
RETURN n
ORDER BY n.name ASC
SKIP 2

scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[2] Start the result from the second row by param
scenarios.description: [2] Start the result from the second row by param
scenarios.query:
MATCH (n)
RETURN n
ORDER BY n.name ASC
SKIP $skipAmount

scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[3] SKIP with an expression that does not depend on variables
scenarios.description: [3] SKIP with an expression that does not depend on variables
scenarios.query:
MATCH (n)
WITH n SKIP toInteger(rand()*9)
WITH count(*) AS count
RETURN count > 0 AS nonEmpty

scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[5] SKIP with an expression that depends on variables should fail
scenarios.description: [5] SKIP with an expression that depends on variables should fail

scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[6] Negative parameter for SKIP should fail
scenarios.description: [6] Negative parameter for SKIP should fail
scenarios.query:
MATCH (p:Person)
RETURN p.name AS name
SKIP $_skip

scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[7] Negative SKIP should fail
scenarios.query:
MATCH (p:Person)
RETURN p.name AS name
SKIP -1

scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[8] Floating point parameter for SKIP should fail
scenarios.description: [8] Floating point parameter for SKIP should fail
scenarios.query:
MATCH (p:Person)
RETURN p.name AS name
SKIP $_limit

scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[9] Floating point SKIP should fail
scenarios.query:
MATCH (p:Person)
RETURN p.name AS name
SKIP 1.5

scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[10] Fail when using non-constants in SKIP
scenarios.description: [10] Fail when using non-constants in SKIP

scenarios.id: ReturnSkipLimit1 - Skip-[11] Fail when using negative value in SKIP
scenarios.description: [11] Fail when using negative value in SKIP
